creator: feld@FreeBSD.org
text:
This only works if you set your default python version to 3.x. That's the current blocker.

If I commit this as-is it's impossible for someone to just "pkg install redshift" and get a working version. I would have to chase all of the python dependencies and make copies of those ports that ensure they are python 3.x specific so there are py3x-foo packages ready to be installed from the mirror.

I'm working on finding a cleaner alternative...
